title: Livestock evacuation loading and destination plan
summary: An early-departure plan for identification, trained loading, vehicle capacity, routes, destination acceptance, feed, water, records, and animals that cannot be moved safely.
content:
An early-departure plan for identification, trained loading, vehicle capacity, routes, destination acceptance, feed, water, records, and animals that cannot be moved safely.

Use the materials as a compact field kit. Work through the steps in order, pause whenever a warning or stop condition applies, and write down the outcome so another person can continue safely.
materials: feed water medicines and handling equipment
steps: Inventory animals by species, number, identification, handling needs, medicines, and transport capacity; confirm destinations before an emergency.
Train loading calmly in advance and inspect floors, gates, ventilation, tyres, lights, tow ratings, partitions, and emergency release equipment.
Leave early when wildfire, flood, extreme heat, or road closure could remove safe loading time; use only routes and destinations confirmed open.
If evacuation is impossible, use the pre-agreed sheltering plan, move animals away from the hazard when safe, provide accessible water, mark the location, and inform authorities.
warnings: Do not attempt first-time loading while fire, floodwater, or panic is already present.
Do not overload trailers or mix incompatible animals.
common_mistakes: Starting Livestock evacuation loading and destination plan without first checking immediate danger and official instructions.
Failing to record time, location, responsible person, and unresolved risks for Livestock evacuation loading and destination plan.
alternatives: Use a paper-only version of Livestock evacuation loading and destination plan when power or connectivity is unavailable.
Assign one trained helper to simplify Livestock evacuation loading and destination plan when the group is stressed or short-handed.
failure_signs: Conditions become more dangerous while carrying out Livestock evacuation loading and destination plan.
Critical information cannot be confirmed or the assigned helper cannot continue Livestock evacuation loading and destination plan safely.
when_not_to_use: Do not continue Livestock evacuation loading and destination plan when it requires entering an unstable, contaminated, flooded, burning, electrically hazardous, or officially closed area.
Do not improvise medical, electrical, structural, or rescue actions beyond your training while using Livestock evacuation loading and destination plan.
short_term: ['Within the first operational period, Livestock evacuation loading and destination plan should produce a dated status, one responsible person, and a safe next action.', 'Repeat the essential check for Livestock evacuation loading and destination plan whenever conditions, official advice, or available resources change.']
long_term: ['Include lessons from Livestock evacuation loading and destination plan in the household or community emergency plan and resupply list.', 'Review the records from Livestock evacuation loading and destination plan after the event and correct gaps before the next emergency.']
if_method_fails: ['If Livestock evacuation loading and destination plan cannot be completed, move to the safest verified location, notify the responsible contact, and record what remains unresolved.', 'Use official emergency services or qualified specialists when the limits of Livestock evacuation loading and destination plan are reached.']
environment_notes: ['Adapt Livestock evacuation loading and destination plan to local terrain, weather, building type, transport access, language, disability needs, and official restrictions.', 'In Greece, use 112 for urgent emergencies and follow Civil Protection or local-authority instructions relevant to Livestock evacuation loading and destination plan.']
